figures.caption: Figure 1: System-Level Architecture of Dexterous Hands: Actuation, Transmission, and Multimodal Perception
figures.caption_ko: Dexterous hand를 actuation, transmission, multimodal perception의 system-level architecture로 분해한다.

figures.caption: Figure 2: Research Workflow on Dexterous Hand
figures.caption_ko: dexterous hand 연구를 task planning, policy training, deployment feedback loop로 정리한 workflow.

figures.caption: Figure 3: Research on Dexterous Hand
figures.caption_ko: in-hand manipulation, grasp/pick-and-place, tool operation, human interaction, bimanual manipulation으로 dexterous hand 연구를 나누는 지형도.

figures.caption: Figure 6: Overview of diffusion policies connecting multimodal perception to diverse dexterous manipulation tasks. The generative policy denoises action states into smooth control trajectories for varying task requirements.
figures.caption_ko: multimodal perception을 diffusion policy로 dexterous manipulation trajectory에 연결하는 구조.

figures.caption: Figure 7: General architecture of a Vision-Language-Action (VLA) foundation model. Multimodal inputs (RGB/Depth and natural language instructions) are processed through a central Transformer model, which outputs low-level joint angle and torque commands for dexterous execution.
figures.caption_ko: RGB/Depth와 자연어 지시를 Transformer 기반 VLA가 joint angle/torque command로 변환하는 일반 구조.

figures.caption: Figure 8: Tool Use & Device Operation Timeline
figures.caption_ko: tool use 및 device operation 연구의 시간축.

cover_caption: Figure 1: System-Level Architecture of Dexterous Hands: Actuation, Transmission, and Multimodal Perception
source_title: Towards Robotic Dexterous Hand Intelligence: A Survey

ai_summary.one_liner: A holistic survey that connects dexterous hand hardware, sensing, control/learning methods, datasets, and evaluation into one developmental map.
ai_summary.problem: Dexterous hand studies differ in embodiment, sensors, task assumptions, data sources, and evaluation protocols, making progress hard to compare systematically.
ai_summary.solution: The survey analyzes actuation, transmission, perception, representative hands, task categories, policy-learning paradigms, datasets, modalities, and evaluation practice under one taxonomy.
ai_summary.key_result: Its main output is a structured map of the field: hardware choices constrain policy assumptions, learning methods depend on data/evaluation design, and robust deployment requires closing gaps in sensing, sim-to-real transfer, benchmarks, and safety.
limitations: The survey is taxonomy-heavy and does not provide a unified benchmark or meta-analysis across all cited methods.
Fast-moving 2026 dexterous-hand and humanoid-hand releases may outpace the static survey snapshot.

research_gaps: Hardware, tactile sensing, and policy learning are often optimized separately.
General dexterous VLA systems still need reliable real-time force and safety grounding.
future_directions: Co-design of hand hardware, tactile sensing, and policy learning.
Standardized dexterous manipulation datasets and evaluation protocols.
Robust sim-to-real pipelines for high-DoF contact-rich hands.
